essence: Dark Factory has failed its core promise of building itself — it is fragile, makes patchwork fixes without architectural thinking, and leaves David managing the process — so he weighs manually building AppyRadar/Switchboard/WatchTower first, re-reviewing every doc and commit for drift, unifying docs+memories across machines, and spending scarce Fable 5 credits sparingly with build delegation to the M4 Mini.

actions.text: Re-review every document and commit for documentation drift and build a Q&A backlog to get back on track
Consolidate documentation and machine-local memories into one aligned file set, commit, and delegate Fable 5 task runs to the M4 Mini

essence: David plans a ~2-week Vietnam trip, expecting to leave Thailand before his 90-day report rather than after, and asks Claude to save Dad's latest info to a file and confirm whether it has access to his email.

actions.text: Save Dad's latest travel information into a file
Confirm whether Claude has access to David's email

essence: David directs that a provenance chain be kept for the current cortex/brain work so the whole exercise can be reproduced when the problem is revisited in roughly two weeks.

actions.text: Keep a provenance chain so the work can be redone at the two-week re-look

essence: David tells the agent to do research and give him clarity plus a decision framing before offering any suggestions on the matter at hand.
David asks whether the work done so far has actually improved brain cohesiveness between his two Macs, and whether AWB lives inside the brains system or is a separate folder altogether.

David readies Spec C for a coding handoff (asking for an Osmani-skill write-up, Linear update, and a paste-ready handover message), demands repos and brains be verified in sync between M4 Pro and M4 Mini including Roamy's remote pushes, and frames a coming gap analysis of cortex/portable-brain against competitor brains, while clarifying whether Spec D is only for Arcana KAD/KBDE.

actions.text: Update Linear for the Spec C coding handoff and produce a paste-ready handover message
Verify repos, brains, and related files are synchronized both ways between M4 Pro and M4 Mini and report gaps
Check brains are up to date with remote and that local brain knowledge is pushed

essence: David scopes Brain Core / Cortex Core spec work: Claude can likely run in Australia via Amazon Bedrock but data residency for NDIS participant data must be independently researched, a background agent should explain why KAD uses a brain provider while KBDE calls directly (one is wrong), upstream brain-related repos need refreshing before gap analysis, and the KAD-to-KyberBot change is just a rename, not a technology shift.

actions.text: Research Australian data residency for Claude on Amazon Bedrock as it applies to NDIS participant data
Run a background agent on why KAD uses a brain provider while KBDE calls directly, and resolve the inconsistency
Verify upstream brain-related repos (Jebra etc.) are up to date
Prepare Osmani-style specs for Cortex Core / Brain Core

essence: Probing suspicious look-alike 'analysis dots' in AngelEye (possibly generated noise rather than real brain data), David flags a boundary violation — the file-system reconcile living inside the portable brain that talks to a database — and calls for a background deep-think on architecture plus a shared diff tool usable by KAD, KBDE, and iKANA clients, possibly centralized in KyberAgent core or Cortex core.

actions.text: Background task: review all portable-brain packages and propose a better architecture for the reconcile/diff responsibility
Design a diffing tool shared by KAD, KBDE, and iKANA clients (duplicate vs centralize decision)
Prepare a detailed handover document before compaction (session at 69%)

essence: David reports the brain's missing-file count has ballooned from 42 to 375 between tests, suspects probe queries return gaps because data exists only in files and not in memory, routes separate handovers back to the spec-writing and probe-running systems, and says the release is not yet useful as delivered.

actions.text: Produce handover documents for the spec-writing and probe-running systems so probes account for the 375 missing files
Determine whether the release can proceed after the merger completes

essence: David asks whether a workflow is already running dot probes and whether issuing a new Spec B ticket would conflict with another window running on main.

Driving the coding workflow forward, David asks why a workflow failed, wants the handover rewritten for the coding window, files and wave one committed with audit trails confirmed, Spec B researched for relevance to the Cortex system before he starts it, and wave two on the probes run later in a background agent (context at 65%).

actions.text: Commit the requested files and wave one with audit trails confirmed
Research whether Spec B is also relevant for the Cortex system and drop the idea note into cortex
Run wave two on the probes in a background agent after coding starts
